text: Which BS 7671 chapter sets out protection against electric shock, including ADS and RCD requirements?
In TN systems, what is the maximum disconnection time for final circuits ≤ 32 A at 230 V?
In TT systems, what is the maximum disconnection time for final circuits ≤ 32 A at 230 V?
What IP rating is required for equipment installed in bathroom Zone 0?
What is the typical SELV voltage limit permitted in bathroom Zone 0 for luminaires?
Which breaker curve is most commonly used for general domestic final circuits?
Which devices provide time-delayed RCD characteristics to aid discrimination (selectivity)?
Which chapter of BS 7671 covers protection against thermal effects (including fire risk)?
Which section of BS 7671 deals with locations containing a bath or shower?
Under Section 443, when are SPDs generally required in domestic installations?
options: Required unless the owner documents acceptance of risk and consequences are low
Only if there is PV installed
Only if the consumer unit is plastic
correct_answer: Required unless the owner documents acceptance of risk and consequences are low

text: Which SPD type is installed at the origin when there is a risk of direct lightning currents (e.g. LPS or overhead lines)?
Main protective bonding conductor sizing for TN-C-S (PME) systems shall follow Table 54.8 and shall not be less than which copper size?
Which formula is used to size a protective conductor based on fault energy?
What minimum insulation resistance value is generally required for LV final circuits tested at 500 V DC?
Which instrument output current is required for continuity testing of protective conductors (including ring final continuity)?
Which test determines the prospective short-circuit current at the origin?
For ring final circuits, which test method confirms correct ring continuity of line and CPC?
options: R1+R2 at the farthest point only
Cross-connection of ends (r1-rn, r1-r2) and measurement of r1+rn and r1+r2 values around the ring
correct_answer: Cross-connection of ends (r1-rn, r1-r2) and measurement of r1+rn and r1+r2 values around the ring

text: Which regulation group in BS 7671 governs cables concealed in walls and the need for 30 mA RCD protection if < 50 mm deep?
Which RCD type is typically required on general-purpose socket circuits to account for pulsed DC components from appliances?
EV charging points must include which protective feature with respect to PEN conductor faults?
options: Open-PEN fault protection or equivalent means per Section 722
correct_answer: Open-PEN fault protection or equivalent means per Section 722

text: For EV charging equipment, which RCD solution complies with BS 7671 when DC residual current may occur?
options: Type A RCD with 6 mA DC protection (RDC-DD) or Type B RCD
correct_answer: Type A RCD with 6 mA DC protection (RDC-DD) or Type B RCD

text: Voltage drop limits for final circuits are typically which values (excluding distribution) under BS 7671 guidance?
Which chapter of BS 7671 covers selection and erection of wiring systems and cables?
Which devices are intended to detect arc faults and are recommended/required on certain socket circuits in higher-risk residential premises?
Which section of BS 7671 addresses transient overvoltages and SPD selection?
Which section of BS 7671 provides requirements for selection and erection of surge protective devices?
Supplementary bonding in bathrooms is NOT required where which conditions are met?
options: Circuit disconnection times and RCD additional protection are met, and extraneous-conductive-parts are bonded
Only if all circuits are SELV
correct_answer: Circuit disconnection times and RCD additional protection are met, and extraneous-conductive-parts are bonded

text: Which is TRUE about consumer units in domestic premises under current BS 7671?
options: They must be of non-combustible construction (e.g., metal) and suitably enclosed
correct_answer: They must be of non-combustible construction (e.g., metal) and suitably enclosed

text: Which test verifies correct polarity at a socket-outlet (line to line contact, neutral to neutral, CPC to earth)?
Before live testing, safe isolation requires what sequence?
options: Prove the tester, test the circuit, re-prove the tester
correct_answer: Prove the tester, test the circuit, re-prove the tester
